scenarios.title: Common Scenarios
scenarios.subtitle: Get specific guidance for real-life situations

police.title: Stopped by Police
police.description: Know your rights when questioned without reason
police.guidance: You have the right to remain silent and request legal counsel. Police must provide valid grounds for detention.
police.example: In a 2018 Delhi case, the court ruled in favor of a citizen illegally detained without proper documentation.
police.action: Request written documentation and contact a lawyer immediately.

discrimination.title: Workplace Discrimination
discrimination.description: Facing unfair treatment at school or work
discrimination.guidance: Discrimination based on religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth is illegal in India.
discrimination.example: A Mumbai company was fined for refusing employment based on caste in 2020.
discrimination.action: Document incidents and file a complaint with the National Human Rights Commission.

eviction.title: Landlord Issues
eviction.guidance: Landlords must provide written notice as per the rental agreement and cannot forcibly evict you.
eviction.example: In Bangalore, a court stayed eviction proceedings where proper notice wasn't given.
eviction.action: Demand written notice and consult a tenant rights lawyer.

harassment.title: Women Safety
harassment.right: Article 21 - Right to Live with Dignity
harassment.guidance: Sexual harassment is illegal. Women have the right to file FIR and seek police protection.
harassment.example: Supreme Court's Vishaka Guidelines mandate workplace safety committees.
harassment.action: File a police complaint and contact women's helpline 1091.

rights.title: Your Fundamental Rights
rights.subtitle: Know the 6 pillars of Indian democracy

equality.title: Right to Equality
equality.description: Prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth

freedom.title: Right to Freedom
freedom.description: Protects freedoms of speech, assembly, association, movement, residence, and profession

exploitation.title: Right Against Exploitation
exploitation.description: Prohibits trafficking, forced labor, and child labor

religion.title: Right to Freedom of Religion
religion.description: Ensures religious freedom and the right to manage religious institutions

cultural.title: Cultural & Educational Rights
cultural.description: Protects rights of minorities to conserve culture and establish educational institutions

remedies.title: Right to Constitutional Remedies
remedies.description: Guarantees the right to approach courts for enforcement of Fundamental Rights

about.title: Our Mission
about.description: Lawmate's mission is to make legal knowledge accessible and multilingual — so every citizen of India can stand up for their rights.
about.subtitle: We believe every Indian deserves to understand and exercise their constitutional rights.
